topic: In China employee termination: plain-language legal and payroll explainer for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination guide for foreign employers

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Core legal basis for termination, severance exposure, and employer obligations; best anchor for non-local readers.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Provides statutory context for offboarding-related payroll and contribution closure duties.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
sources.fit_note: Useful practical reference for non-local readers on payroll-tax treatment context and annual reconciliation environment.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
sources.fit_note: Adds multinational execution perspective and helps translate legal rules into operational risk language.

consensus: Foreign employers should treat termination in China as a legal-plus-operations process, not just an HR admin task.
Process quality and documentation consistency are central to reducing dispute exposure.
Payroll release decisions should separate uncontested and contested components when uncertainty exists.
uncertainties: City-level evidence expectations and dispute-handling practice can vary.
Some payroll treatment details for specific components can require local confirmation.
English-language summaries may not capture all Chinese-language implementation details.
source_fit_assessment: Source set is sufficient for this structure because it combines primary legal texts (NPC), a practical tax reference (PwC), and multinational implementation analysis (Littler). No weak source was used in this run.
